questions.question: Explain why a tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.
questions.answer: The radius is the shortest distance from the centre to the tangent, hence it is perpendicular.

questions.question: Why can only one tangent be drawn at a point on a circle?
questions.answer: Because only one line can be perpendicular to the radius at a given point.

questions.question: Explain why no tangent can be drawn from a point inside a circle.
questions.answer: Every line through an interior point intersects the circle at two points.

questions.question: Why are two tangents possible from an external point?
questions.answer: Because two lines from the point can touch the circle at exactly one point each.

questions.question: Explain why the lengths of tangents from an external point are equal.
questions.answer: The triangles formed are congruent by RHS congruence rule.

questions.question: What is meant by the length of a tangent? Explain.
questions.answer: It is the distance between the external point and the point of contact.

questions.question: Explain the relation between secant and tangent.
questions.answer: A tangent is a limiting case of a secant when two points coincide.

questions.question: Why is the centre equidistant from all points on the circle?
questions.answer: Because all distances are radii.

questions.question: Explain why the tangent touches the circle only once.
questions.answer: Touching more than once would make it a secant.

questions.question: Why is perpendicular distance shortest?
questions.answer: According to geometry, perpendicular gives minimum distance.

questions.question: Explain the term normal to a circle.
questions.answer: It is the radius through the point of contact of the tangent.

questions.question: Why are parallel tangents possible to a circle?
questions.answer: Because tangents can touch the circle on opposite sides.

questions.question: Explain the uniqueness of tangent at a point.
questions.answer: Only one perpendicular can be drawn at a point.

questions.question: Why is radius called shortest distance to tangent?
questions.answer: Because it is perpendicular to the tangent.

questions.question: Explain the significance of Theorem 10.1.
questions.answer: It establishes the perpendicularity of radius and tangent.

questions.question: Explain the significance of Theorem 10.2.
questions.answer: It proves equality of tangents from an external point.

questions.question: Why is OP common in tangent proof?
questions.answer: It is a common side in both right triangles.

questions.question: Explain why tangent does not intersect the interior.
questions.answer: Because it only touches the boundary of the circle.

questions.question: Explain why a tangent cannot pass through centre.
questions.answer: The centre lies inside the circle.

questions.question: Explain the angle between two tangents.
questions.answer: It is related to the angle subtended at the centre.

questions.question: Why does OP bisect the angle between tangents?
questions.answer: Because the triangles formed are congruent.

questions.question: Explain the role of RHS congruence in tangents.
questions.answer: It proves equality of tangent lengths.

questions.question: Why are radii equal in tangent constructions?
questions.answer: All radii of the same circle are equal.

questions.question: Explain why tangent geometry is rigid.
questions.answer: Because it is based on fixed perpendicular relations.

questions.question: Explain the importance of tangents in circles.
questions.answer: They explain boundary contact behavior.
